name: Chicken Breasts with Chilies and Arroz Blanco
recipeIngredient: 8 Chicken breast halves, skinned and boned
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 large Onion, thinly sliced and separated into rings
2 lbs Anaheim chilies, peeled and seeded, divided or 4 cans (4 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
Season chicken with salt and pepper. In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons Butter and oil over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook until light brown on both sides. Remove from pan and set aside. Cook onion in the same skillet until soft but not brown. Cut all but 3 chilies into strips and add to the onion. Cook over medium heat for 5 minutes, then remove from heat and set aside. In a blender or food processor, blend 3 whole chilies, 1/4 cup milk, and 1/2 teaspoon salt until smooth. Add sour cream and blend a few seconds more. Set aside. Melt the remaining 2 tablespoons Butter in a small saucepan. Blend in flour, then add 3/4 cup Milk and cook until smooth and thickened. Remove from heat and stir in the sour cream mixture. Arrange half the chicken breasts in an oven-safe dish. Top with half the onion chili mixture. Spread with half the sauce. Repeat layers. Cover and bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 25 minutes. Remove from oven, sprinkle with cheese, and return to bake for 3 to 4 minutes more. Serve over beds of fluffy Arroz Blanco. Makes 6 servings.
ARROZ BLANCO:
Melt 2 tablespoons Butter or margarine in a 2 to 3 quart saucepan. Add 1 cup uncooked rice and cook over moderate heat until golden, stirring often. Add 1/2 cup chopped onions and 2 cloves minced garlic; cook until onions are soft but not brown. Add 2 cups chicken broth and 1 teaspoon cumin seed. Bring to a boil, then stir once or twice. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed. Salt to taste.
